prerequisites: An active OnePageCRM account with API access enabled.
Your OnePageCRM API credentials (User ID and API Key), found under the User/Profile icon -> Apps and Integrations -> API -> Configuration tab.
The username value for the connector's required `username` config field.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic Authentication
authMethods.summary: OnePageCRM authenticates API requests with HTTP Basic Auth. This connector supplies the credentials through the `username` config field and the `password` secret field. Retrieve the matching values (User ID and API Key) from the account's Apps and Integrations -> API -> Configuration tab.

setupSteps.title: Locate your API credentials
setupSteps.body: Log into OnePageCRM, then open the User/Profile icon (top right) -> Apps and Integrations -> API. On the Configuration tab you can view the Endpoint URL, User ID, and API Key (expand it with Show API key). These values are used as the username and password for HTTP Basic Auth.

setupSteps.title: Set the username
setupSteps.body: Enter your OnePageCRM Basic Auth identifier into the connector's required `username` field. Per OnePageCRM's docs, the username and password for basic auth come from the Apps -> API -> Configuration tab.

setupSteps.title: Set the password secret
setupSteps.body: Provide the corresponding secret into the connector's `password` field. This is your OnePageCRM API Key / Basic Auth password obtained from the same Configuration tab.

setupSteps.title: Save and test
setupSteps.body: Save the connector configuration. OnePageCRM uses HTTP Basic Auth for every request, so the connector authenticates by sending the username and password together; verify the credentials succeed against the OnePageCRM API.
